prompts.text: High-quality anime style, 2D animation. Warm, cinematic lighting with sunlight filtering through blinds. A tiny man wearing a green hoodie and blue jeans runs across a wooden floor toward a giantess. He climbs up her massive foot and leg, which are covered in black fishnet stockings, using the fishnet holes to climb. Dynamic low-angle camera following his ascent, emphasizing the colossal scale of the giantess. He climbs past her black pleated skirt and white collared shirt, scaling up her dark blue ribbed sweater. The camera pans up to reveal the giantess looking down at him. She has long brown hair, a flower hair clip, and large brown eyes. She wears a highly surprised, wide-eyed expression with a slight blush. Background is a softly blurred bedroom with a music keyboard.

prompts.text: Seedance 2.0 Prompt 15s / 180 BPM / ONE CONTINUOUS SHOT / 360 POV downhill longboard run, viral energy, max chaos. SUBJECTS: First-person downhill longboard rider, front foot, deck nose, and flickering trucks appearing low in frame during drops, slides, and stair gaps. Flower pots, café chairs, scooters, cats, delivery bikes, tourists, and laundry erupt around the rider as sudden obstacles. ENVIRONMENT: Dense Mediterranean cliffside town, whitewashed stair alleys, blue shutters, tiled landings, sea-glow at the horizon, hanging laundry, tangled wires, sun-faded murals, narrow terraces, umbrellas, puddles, warm late afternoon light, deep stone shadows, layered rooftops dropping toward the coast below. MOOD: Adrenaline, max chaos, nonstop downhill street speed. COLOR LOGIC: Hyperreal Pop Look with sun-bleached whites, cobalt accents, coral signage, golden flare. CAMERA DETAILS: 360 action-cam POV, horizon-stable but brutal, nonstop forward drive, minimal roll, heavy stair vibration, sharp side and rear parallax, full-sphere chaos, no release. 0:00–0:03: POV freefalls into a steep stone stairwell, board nose rattling low in frame as the rider lands into brutal vibrating descent. A café server yanks a tray away from the line while walls, balconies, plants, and faces whip past inches away. SFX: sea wind, wheel chatter, stair hits, breath, distant crowd. 0:03–0:05: POV slashes left across a tiny tiled landing, clips past stacked flower pots, then snaps into another stair drop as hanging laundry lashes across the front hemisphere and tourists recoil. SFX: cloth slap, ceramic clink, wheel scrape, crowd gasp. 0:05–0:08: POV hammers the next staircase as a cat darts center frame and pigeons burst upward from a side ledge. The rider speed-check slides, then threads between a parked scooter and iron handrail with almost no clearance. SFX: tire chirp, wings flapping, metal rattle, stair thuds. 0:08–0:10: POV blasts over a short cobblestone gap between stair sections, trucks clacking, then drops straight into the next descending run as delivery bikes streak through the side wrap. SFX: wheel buzz, curb thump, distant horn, crowd echo. 0:10–0:13: POV attacks two tight landings and another steep stepped lane, every hit jolting the sphere as rooftops, awnings, church bells, alley mouths, and flashes of sea curl around the viewer. SFX: air rush, heavy thump, stair chatter, wind buffeting. 0:13–0:15: POV rips to a cliff overlook and throws into a savage stand-up slide, dust and tiny pebbles spraying across the lower frame while the glowing sea-town skyline blooms around the full sphere. SFX: wheel screech, gravel spray, crowd roar. Hyperreal, continuous-shot chaos, no text, no logos.

prompts.text: Medieval cat master: cute first, survival later (created with Seedance 2.0 on @nemovideoai ) Prompt share Seedance 2 Prompt (15 seconds total, each shot ~0.6–1.2s) Cinematic fantasy dark-comedy action, medieval European castle bedroom, warm candlelight vs cold moonlight contrast, dramatic shadows, ultra-dynamic camera, exaggerated character animation, high-detail textures, fast-paced editing, humorous tone. S1 (0.9s) Wide shot: A chubby orange cat in full medieval European knight armor stands beside a luxurious canopy bed inside a castle bedroom, moonlight spilling through wooden windows. S2 (0.8s) Close-up: On the bed, a white cat princess sleeps messily, loudly snoring, one paw casually picking her nose in an unrefined, comedic manner. S3 (0.9s) Medium shot: The orange cat shakes her urgently, armor clanking, trying to wake her up, visibly frustrated. S4 (0.7s) Close-up: The princess continues snoring, completely unfazed, bubble forming from her nose. S5 (0.8s) Cut to window: Outside the wooden window, a pair of giant glowing eyes suddenly appears in the darkness. S6 (0.9s) Reveal shot: A pink fire-breathing dragon presses its face near the window, exhaling faint smoke, eyes wide and menacing. S7 (0.8s) Reaction shot: The orange cat freezes, then instantly turns serious, gripping his longsword. S8 (1.0s) Action burst: With a powerful kick, the orange cat smashes through the wooden window, splinters flying outward. S9 (1.0s) Balcony wide shot: The cat lands on the stone balcony, facing the hovering pink dragon under moonlight. S10 (1.0s) Combat: The cat lunges forward, slashing rapidly with the longsword, sparks and motion blur streaks. S11 (0.8s) Twist: The dragon suddenly opens its mouth and bites down, swallowing the sword whole. S12 (0.9s) Close-up: The orange cat pauses, then instantly switches expression, huge sparkling eyes, puffed cheeks, exaggerated ultra-cute innocent face. S13 (1.0s) Reaction: The dragon’s fierce expression melts into confusion and softness, flames dying down. S14 (1.0s) Punchline action: In a split-second, the orange cat snaps back to normal and kicks the dragon hard, sending it tumbling off the balcony into a deep abyss. S15 (1.2s) Final close-up: The orange cat turns to camera, smirks proudly, and gives a playful left-eye wink, armor gleaming in moonlight. Motion & Effects: Fast-paced comedic timing, strong motion blur, dynamic camera swings (orbit, low-angle, snap zooms), particle effects (wood splinters, smoke, sparks), expressive character animation, seamless flow, high-impact yet humorous finish. #sd2 #seedance #CatsLover #aivideo

prompts.text: The chase across the Moon! Prompt on Seedance 2 : Open on a majestic lunar landscape under black sky, silver dust exploding under the hooves of a lone white horse galloping across crater edges. Earth looms gigantic above the horizon, blue and fragile. The camera races beside the horse, matching its speed, then swings ahead and backward in one fluid motion, revealing a hooded rider clutching something bright against their chest. The pursuit intensifies — behind them, distant headlights appear, bouncing over the crater field like hunters closing in. The camera rises high to show the horse crossing impossible terrain, then dives again as the rider approaches a cliff edge where the moon’s surface drops into darkness. With nowhere left to run, the rider stands in the stirrups and hurls the glowing object skyward. The camera follows it up through silent lunar night — and the reveal hits: the moon, horse, hunters, everything is part of an elaborate carousel in an abandoned amusement park. Generated and upscaled on Runway

prompts.text: Static close-up shot. A realistic human hand interacts with a flat 2D animated character of Furina — a blue and white dress anime girl with long light blue hair — inside a clear glass cup placed on a wooden table. [0-2s] The hand pinches Furina by the head and lowers her into the glass; she grabs the rim with both arms and resists, refusing to go in, while the fingers keep pressing her down. [2-4s] She loses grip and slides down the glass wall, desperately kicking and clawing at the wall trying to climb back up, but keeps slipping back to the bottom, with a surprised yelp. [4-7s] She gives up and slumps at the bottom, eyes shut, two exaggerated wide noodle-tears streaming down her cheeks, crying dramatically. [7-9s] She suddenly leaps up, shaking both fists at her sides furiously, tears still on her face, yelling angrily upward in protest — expression both crying and fuming, super cute. [9-11s] The finger reaches in and gently pats her head; she immediately stops, sits still obediently, hands clasped on her chest, looking up at the finger with big teary puppy eyes. [11-13s] The fingers pinch her head again and lift her straight up out of the glass; halfway up she tilts her head slightly, eyes half-lidded, a small smug tsundere smile on her lips. [13-15s] Fully lifted out and suspended in the air, her eyes open wide and she breaks into a big bright energetic grin, hands spread open happily, frame holds steady. AR mixed-media style: flat 2D cartoon character with full physical interaction with a real 3D glass — sliding and climbing along the glass wall, realistic glass refractions, cast shadows, and wall contact all rendered naturally. Soft warm natural lighting, super cute and heartwarming mood. #furina #genshinimpact

prompts.text: Cat master’s mom said “no funny business” 😼 (made with Seedance 2 on @nemovideoai ) Prompt share ✏️👇 The Homework Cat vs the Ultimate Distraction A cinematic chaotic comedy short. An orange cat in a white short-sleeve shirt and dark blue shorts sits at a cluttered desk in a small bedroom, frowning at a mountain of homework. He fidgets, sighs dramatically, and keeps sneaking glances at his glowing phone. His opponent is not a monster—but an unstoppable force of distraction, laziness, and bad decisions. The setting flows from messy study desk → bedroom doorway → time-skipping waiting scene → chaotic living room → back to desk → messy bed finale. 0–3 seconds: Close-up: pencil tapping rapidly. The orange cat groans, slumps, then secretly slides his phone out under the desk. Door creaks open—cut to the white cat mother in pink home pajamas, holding a steaming can of cat food like a sacred offering. The orange cat instantly jolts upright, panic face, thumbs still mid-scroll. Quick cuts: glowing phone screen, suspicious mom eyes, drifting steam from the can. 3–6 seconds: The orange cat springs up awkwardly, blurting an excuse—points toward the hallway. He speed-walks out stiffly. The mom slowly sits at the desk, placing the steaming can down. Silence. Cut to wall clock: 18:00. Rapid comedic time-lapse—clock hands spinning violently to 19:30. Close-up: mom’s face shifts from calm → confused → annoyed → eye twitch. 6–9 seconds: The mom stands abruptly and marches out. Hard cut to living room: chaos explosion. The orange cat is on the floor, fully immersed, playing with a robot toy battle—sound effects, exaggerated movements. Wide shot: toys scattered everywhere like a battlefield. Close-ups: spinning robot arm, cat’s excited grin, tiny explosion toy effect. The mom enters frame—FREEZE moment—then erupts in anger. 9–12 seconds: Rapid cuts: mom shouting, finger pointing, orange cat shrinking. The orange cat trudges back to the room, shoulders slumped in defeat. He sits at the desk, stares blankly at a multiple-choice worksheet (YES / NO questions). Suddenly—evil genius spark. He grabs pencil and starts ticking ALL YES at lightning speed. Close-ups: furious checkmarks, mischievous grin, paper filling instantly. 12–15 seconds: Victory moment—he leaps onto the bed with the canned food. Messy feast: food splattering, crumbs everywhere, total chaos. Slow-motion comedic chewing, absolute satisfaction. Door creaks—mom peeks through a tiny gap. She sees EVERYTHING: messy bed, unfinished logic, food disaster. She pauses... then just sighs and shakes her head. Final shot: A single worksheet page on the desk—every answer marked YES. A drop of sauce falls onto it. #sd2 #seedance #aivideo #CatsLover

Messy feast: food splattering, crumbs everywhere, total chaos. Slow-motion comedic chewing, absolute satisfaction. Door creaks—mom peeks through a tiny gap. She sees EVERYTHING: messy bed, unfinished logic, food disaster. She pauses... then just sighs and shakes her head. Final shot: A single worksheet page on the desk—every answer marked YES. A drop of sauce falls onto it.

Cat master’s mom said “no funny business” 😼
(made with Seedance 2 on @nemovideoai  )
Prompt share ✏️👇

The Homework Cat vs the Ultimate Distraction
A cinematic chaotic comedy short.

An orange cat in a white short-sleeve shirt and dark blue shorts sits at a cluttered desk in a small bedroom, frowning at a mountain of homework. He fidgets, sighs dramatically, and keeps sneaking glances at his glowing phone. His opponent is not a monster—but an unstoppable force of distraction, laziness, and bad decisions.

The setting flows from messy study desk → bedroom doorway → time-skipping waiting scene → chaotic living room → back to desk → messy bed finale.

0–3 seconds:
Close-up: pencil tapping rapidly. The orange cat groans, slumps, then secretly slides his phone out under the desk.
Door creaks open—cut to the white cat mother in pink home pajamas, holding a steaming can of cat food like a sacred offering.
The orange cat instantly jolts upright, panic face, thumbs still mid-scroll.
Quick cuts: glowing phone screen, suspicious mom eyes, drifting steam from the can.

3–6 seconds:
The orange cat springs up awkwardly, blurting an excuse—points toward the hallway.
He speed-walks out stiffly.
The mom slowly sits at the desk, placing the steaming can down. Silence.
Cut to wall clock: 18:00.
Rapid comedic time-lapse—clock hands spinning violently to 19:30.
Close-up: mom’s face shifts from calm → confused → annoyed → eye twitch.

6–9 seconds:
The mom stands abruptly and marches out.
Hard cut to living room: chaos explosion.
The orange cat is on the floor, fully immersed, playing with a robot toy battle—sound effects, exaggerated movements.
Wide shot: toys scattered everywhere like a battlefield.
Close-ups: spinning robot arm, cat’s excited grin, tiny explosion toy effect.
The mom enters frame—FREEZE moment—then erupts in anger.

9–12 seconds:
Rapid cuts: mom shouting, finger pointing, orange cat shrinking.
The orange cat trudges back to the room, shoulders slumped in defeat.
He sits at the desk, stares blankly at a multiple-choice worksheet (YES / NO questions).
Suddenly—evil genius spark.
He grabs pencil and starts ticking ALL YES at lightning speed.
Close-ups: furious checkmarks, mischievous grin, paper filling instantly.

12–15 seconds:
Victory moment—he leaps onto the bed with the canned food.
Messy feast: food splattering, crumbs everywhere, total chaos.
Slow-motion comedic chewing, absolute satisfaction.
Door creaks—mom peeks through a tiny gap.
She sees EVERYTHING: messy bed, unfinished logic, food disaster.
She pauses… then just sighs and shakes her head.

Final shot:
A single worksheet page on the desk—every answer marked YES.
A drop of sauce falls onto it.

prompts.text: Seedance 2 is actually insane. Hollywood is cooked. Made this in 2 minutes with ONE prompt. No edits. No cuts. No VFX team. Just text. We are so early it's unfair. Prompt 👇 "Ramen Samurai" — Studio Ghibli AnimeTop-down bird's-eye view of a steaming bowl of ramen on a wooden table at a rainy Tokyo night stall. Camera crash-zooms INTO the bowl — we plunge through broth, noodles become rivers, the egg yolk becomes a setting sun, and a tiny samurai made of seaweed rides a noodle-wave. Whip pan up to reveal the old chef smiling, steam curling like dragons around him. Hand-drawn, warm amber palette, painterly rain, Joe Hisaishi piano, ambient ramen slurps.

prompts.text: A small white mushroom-eared creature is hurled forward from outstretched hands. It tumbles toward the camera, its white body swelling until it fills the entire frame — white flash. Chase camera locks behind the mushroom-eared creature as it rockets forward and slams into a wall of red crystalline armor. On impact it explodes — cyan crystal shards and square pixel-particles burst radially. The creature splits into several identical copies that scatter in every direction. The camera pulls straight up, revealing a circular red arena from overhead. The copies ricochet off surfaces in erratic bouncing arcs, each landing triggering a magenta or blue starburst flash. A white variant joins mid-sequence, trailing blue beam lines. The bouncing grows chaotic with overlapping hit effects. One mushroom-eared creature arcs directly past the lens, its body blocking the frame — wipe to black. A character stands behind a large golden wheel covered in ornate engravings. Both hands grip the wheel's rim and push — the wheel spins. The character grins while watching it turn. The camera starts tight on the character's hands and the spinning wheel, then slowly widens. Red carnival lamps ring the stage. Gold sparkles drift upward. Small white and black mushroom-eared creatures sit in the foreground as an audience, arms raised. The spinning golden wheel glows brighter frame by frame, bleaching to searing white. All figures become silhouettes against the expanding light. The camera pushes IN toward the light source — total whiteout. White flash erupts. A warm-colored blast wave expands outward from center screen. Smaller mushroom-eared creatures launch outward from the center in every direction. The character strikes a double peace sign as multicolored confetti rains from above.

prompts.text: Mockumentary (Vlog Style) As long as it can be imagined and described accurately and clearly, I feel like Seedance 2.0 can generate it all Seedance 2.0 prompt Style: Mockumentary (Vlog Style), hyper-realism, fixed camera real-shot feel, natural light, suspense comedy vibe, mobile phone vertical screen shooting texture Duration: 15 seconds Scene: Ordinary family living room, in front of white walls, afternoon sidelight streaming in from the window, yoga mat on the floor, clear human shadows projected on the wall Characters: Female lead @image1 , wearing a sports tank top and yoga pants, ponytail, recording a dance video against a phone tripod [00:00-00:05] Shot 1: Everyday Setup (Normalcy / Fixed Camera) Frame: Fixed camera medium shot, phone selfie perspective. Action: Female lead makes a heart gesture to the camera, then starts dancing a simple popular TikTok routine, movements a bit clumsy, occasionally glancing at the phone on the floor for cues. Key Details: At this point, the shadow on the wall is completely normal, perfectly in sync with her movements. Background is quiet, only the music from the Bluetooth speaker playing. Atmosphere: Utterly everyday, no hints of anything unusual. [00:05-00:11] Shot 2: BUG Emerges (The Glitch / Same Fixed Camera) Action: Female lead repeats the same dance segment, and midway through a turn— High-Energy Moment (Core Punch): The shadow on the wall suddenly **doesn't follow the turn**, instead switching on its own to a completely different set of high-difficulty street dance moves (Breaking Power Move), the shadow executing a flawless Thomas full spin on the wall, smooth and fluid. Female lead hasn't noticed yet, keeps dancing her simple steps, but the shadow has fully broken sync, going wild with a solo on the wall. Director's Note: The shadow's dance skills should clearly outclass the body. The bigger the contrast in their moves, the stronger the comedy—body doing the "I Love You" hand gesture dance, shadow pulling off windmill floor moves. [00:11-00:15] Shot 3: Comedy Reversal (The Punchline / Same Fixed Camera) Action: Female lead finishes dancing and looks back at the phone replay, spots the shadow's anomaly, whips her head around to glare at the wall. Result: The shadow has now reverted to normal—obediently mirroring every one of her movements. Female lead suspiciously raises her right hand slowly, shadow raises its right hand too. She quickly switches to left hand—shadow follows without a hitch. Final Freeze: Female lead with a "Am I losing my mind?" expression straight to the camera, but in the last 0.5 seconds before the frame ends, the shadow sneaks in a peace sign gesture the instant she looks at the lens. Frame freezes on this beat.

prompts.text: Extreme close-up from low angle. The torso twists away from the draw side — the left shoulder drops forward, the right shoulder pulls back, weight loading into the rear hip. Magenta energy arcs swirl around the torso, building intensity. A bright flash ignites at the sword hilt.The body uncoils — explosive rush directly toward the camera with heavy motion blur and diagonal speed streaks. The face fills the frame for one instant, eyes locked forward — then the drawn blade sweeps past in a magenta flash and the figure is already behind the camera, shrinking to a distant silhouette against the dark horizon.The sky erupts with criss-crossing sword flash lines — each slash cuts diagonally across the full width of the sky, intersecting other slashes at random angles. Each line appears as a thick bright beam — white-blue core with magenta edges — then gradually thins and fades to a thin pink trace. New lines continuously appear while older ones dissipate. The layered effect builds to peak density before slowly subsiding.The camera rushes forward to catch up. A glowing blade trail sweeps across the lens. The figure stands in noto posture — right hand raised above the left shoulder holding the sword at a diagonal, left hand at waist level guiding the blade toward the scabbard opening.The blade gleams with a bright star-burst sparkle at its midpoint. Inch by inch over several seconds, the right hand lowers — the glowing sword descends in a slow, deliberate arc toward the waiting left hand. The gleam intensifies at the point where blade meets scabbard.The instant the blade seats — a massive magenta-white explosion of light detonates from behind. Radial beams shoot outward in every direction. The figure becomes a pure silhouette against the overwhelming brightness.Within the light burst, the kanji 斬 materializes in bold calligraphic brushstrokes — dark ink against the blinding glow. Ink-splash drip effects trail from the base of each stroke. A bright horizontal magenta beam slices through the center of the kanji, and the character fragments along the cut line — upper strokes drift upward, lower strokes drift downward, as if space itself has been bisected. The magenta glow gradually dims. The fractured kanji holds its split position as the light fades to a twilight afterglow.

prompt:  
Extreme close-up from low angle. The torso twists away from the draw side — the left shoulder drops forward, the right shoulder pulls back, weight loading into the rear hip. Magenta energy arcs swirl around the torso, building intensity. A bright flash ignites at the sword hilt.The body uncoils — explosive rush directly toward the camera with heavy motion blur and diagonal speed streaks. The face fills the frame for one instant, eyes locked forward — then the drawn blade sweeps past in a magenta flash and the figure is already behind the camera, shrinking to a distant silhouette against the dark horizon.The sky erupts with criss-crossing sword flash lines — each slash cuts diagonally across the full width of the sky, intersecting other slashes at random angles. Each line appears as a thick bright beam — white-blue core with magenta edges — then gradually thins and fades to a thin pink trace. New lines continuously appear while older ones dissipate. The layered effect builds to peak density before slowly subsiding.The camera rushes forward to catch up. A glowing blade trail sweeps across the lens. The figure stands in noto posture — right hand raised above the left shoulder holding the sword at a diagonal, left hand at waist level guiding the blade toward the scabbard opening.The blade gleams with a bright star-burst sparkle at its midpoint. Inch by inch over several seconds, the right hand lowers — the glowing sword descends in a slow, deliberate arc toward the waiting left hand. The gleam intensifies at the point where blade meets scabbard.The instant the blade seats — a massive magenta-white explosion of light detonates from behind. Radial beams shoot outward in every direction. The figure becomes a pure silhouette against the overwhelming brightness.Within the light burst, the kanji 斬 materializes in bold calligraphic brushstrokes — dark ink against the blinding glow. Ink-splash drip effects trail from the base of each stroke. A bright horizontal magenta beam slices through the center of the kanji, and the character fragments along the cut line — upper strokes drift upward, lower strokes drift downward, as if space itself has been bisected. The magenta glow gradually dims. The fractured kanji holds its split position as the light fades to a twilight afterglow.

prompts.text: Seedance2.0 機体の高速移動 prompt 针对您提供的巨大机甲 <<<Image1>>> (RAY-G),我为您设计了一份时长 15秒、风格极其干练、时髦(Stylish)且充满重力无视感的高速移动视频脚本。 这份提示词融合了《机动战士高达:水星的魔女》的灵动感与《金属装备》的机械冷硬风,重点在于**“瞬间爆发力”与“流线型轨迹”**。 视频生成脚本:【RAY-G: 极速切点 (Point Break)】 总时长: 15秒 风格标签: 极简主义、硬核机甲、高帧率动作、流体动力学、赛璐璐高级感。 (00:00 - 03:00) 序幕:静止的动量 (Static Potential) 镜头: 极近景(Extreme Close-up),斜角构图。 画面: 画面中心是 <<<Image1>>> 漆黑磨砂装甲上的橙色线条。线条开始像血管一样有节奏地剧烈闪烁,发出高频的嗡鸣声。 特效: 空气因高温产生剧烈扭曲(Heat Haze),背景的海面保持极度的静谧,形成动静反差。 [Prompt / 提示词]: 极近景,斜角构图。聚焦于机甲 <<<Image1>>> 的黑色磨砂装甲。鲜艳的橙色线条像呼吸灯一样高频闪烁。装甲缝隙喷射出极细的白色高压蒸汽。环境背景模糊。极简主义色彩,电影级质感,金属冷硬风格。 (03:00 - 06:00) 瞬发:突破音障 (Instant Acceleration) 镜头: 侧面追踪镜头(Side Tracking Shot),伴随强烈的拉伸感(FOV Shift)。 动作: 机甲 @image2 的鸟脚状后肢猛地一踏,脚踝处的滑轮(Land Spinner)高速旋转。瞬间从静止变为超音速,海面被切开一道笔直的白浪。 特效: 产生圆锥形的音爆云(Sonic Boom),橙色残影在空中拉出一道锋利的直线。 [Prompt / 提示词]: 侧面动态追踪。机甲 @image2 瞬间爆发起跑。足部滑轮高速旋转,在海面上切开巨大的V形浪花。超音速冲击波云。极速运动模糊,机甲拉出长长的橙色轨迹残影。空间拉伸感,震撼的加速度视觉。 (06:00 - 10:00) 垂直机动:重力反转 (Vertical Evasion) 镜头: 动态环绕升降镜头(Crane & Orbit)。 动作: 机甲奔向一座半毁的沿海巨塔,完全不减速,直接顺着垂直的墙面向上冲刺。它在垂直墙面上进行了一个华丽的180度螺旋转身,避开假想的攻击。 特效: 碎裂的玻璃碎片在空中飞舞,折射出夕阳的橙光。 [Prompt / 提示词]: 动态环绕镜头。机甲 <<<Image1>>> 在垂直建筑物表面高速冲刺,无视重力。身体进行优雅的螺旋旋转(Barrel Roll)。碎片飞溅,反射夕阳橙光。高对比度阴影,赛璐璐动画风格,极具张力的动作设计。 (10:00 - 13:00) 高光:战术滑行 (Tactical Slide) 镜头: 极低角度(Low Angle)对准足部。 动作: 机甲从高处跃下,足部在海面上进行长距离的漂移滑行。身体压低至极限,呈现掠食者般的姿态。 特效: 巨量的水雾被激起,在阳光下形成彩虹。 [Prompt / 提示词]: 极低角度。机甲 <<<Image1>>> 在海面上低重心漂移滑行。巨大的白色浪花与水雾。掠食者姿态,机械关节细致运作。夕阳余晖,金属反光,色彩饱和度极高,极致的画面纯净感。 (13:00 - 15:00) 尾声:单眼凝视 (The Glare) 镜头: 正面近景,逐渐变焦。 动作: 机甲滑行停止,身体由于惯性轻微晃动。头部导向板打开,中心那颗发光的青绿色单眼(Monocular)猛地看向镜头。 特效: 画面最后瞬间闪过一个“LOCK ON”的橙色UI界面,随即切黑。 [Prompt / 提示词]: 正面近景。机甲 @image2 停止滑行,蒸汽散去。头部青绿色单眼亮起,锐利冷酷。屏幕中心出现橙色锁定UI。电影级运镜,画面骤然切黑。

prompts.tweet_text:
Seedance2.0
機体の高速移動

prompt

针对您提供的巨大机甲 <<<Image1>>>   (RAY-G)，我为您设计了一份时长 15秒、风格极其干练、时髦（Stylish）且充满重力无视感的高速移动视频脚本。

这份提示词融合了《机动战士高达：水星的魔女》的灵动感与《金属装备》的机械冷硬风，重点在于**“瞬间爆发力”与“流线型轨迹”**。

视频生成脚本：【RAY-G: 极速切点 (Point Break)】
总时长： 15秒

风格标签： 极简主义、硬核机甲、高帧率动作、流体动力学、赛璐璐高级感。

(00:00 - 03:00) 序幕：静止的动量 (Static Potential)
镜头： 极近景（Extreme Close-up），斜角构图。

画面： 画面中心是 <<<Image1>>>   漆黑磨砂装甲上的橙色线条。线条开始像血管一样有节奏地剧烈闪烁，发出高频的嗡鸣声。

特效： 空气因高温产生剧烈扭曲（Heat Haze），背景的海面保持极度的静谧，形成动静反差。

[Prompt / 提示词]:

极近景，斜角构图。聚焦于机甲 <<<Image1>>>   的黑色磨砂装甲。鲜艳的橙色线条像呼吸灯一样高频闪烁。装甲缝隙喷射出极细的白色高压蒸汽。环境背景模糊。极简主义色彩，电影级质感，金属冷硬风格。

(03:00 - 06:00) 瞬发：突破音障 (Instant Acceleration)
镜头： 侧面追踪镜头（Side Tracking Shot），伴随强烈的拉伸感（FOV Shift）。

动作： 机甲 
@image2
 的鸟脚状后肢猛地一踏，脚踝处的滑轮（Land Spinner）高速旋转。瞬间从静止变为超音速，海面被切开一道笔直的白浪。

特效： 产生圆锥形的音爆云（Sonic Boom），橙色残影在空中拉出一道锋利的直线。

[Prompt / 提示词]:

侧面动态追踪。机甲 
@image2
 瞬间爆发起跑。足部滑轮高速旋转，在海面上切开巨大的V形浪花。超音速冲击波云。极速运动模糊，机甲拉出长长的橙色轨迹残影。空间拉伸感，震撼的加速度视觉。

(06:00 - 10:00) 垂直机动：重力反转 (Vertical Evasion)
镜头： 动态环绕升降镜头（Crane & Orbit）。

动作： 机甲奔向一座半毁的沿海巨塔，完全不减速，直接顺着垂直的墙面向上冲刺。它在垂直墙面上进行了一个华丽的180度螺旋转身，避开假想的攻击。

特效： 碎裂的玻璃碎片在空中飞舞，折射出夕阳的橙光。

[Prompt / 提示词]:

动态环绕镜头。机甲 <<<Image1>>>   在垂直建筑物表面高速冲刺，无视重力。身体进行优雅的螺旋旋转（Barrel Roll）。碎片飞溅，反射夕阳橙光。高对比度阴影，赛璐璐动画风格，极具张力的动作设计。

(10:00 - 13:00) 高光：战术滑行 (Tactical Slide)
镜头： 极低角度（Low Angle）对准足部。

动作： 机甲从高处跃下，足部在海面上进行长距离的漂移滑行。身体压低至极限，呈现掠食者般的姿态。

特效： 巨量的水雾被激起，在阳光下形成彩虹。

[Prompt / 提示词]:

极低角度。机甲 <<<Image1>>>   在海面上低重心漂移滑行。巨大的白色浪花与水雾。掠食者姿态，机械关节细致运作。夕阳余晖，金属反光，色彩饱和度极高，极致的画面纯净感。

prompts.text: 無重力アクションシーン すごくいい感じに表現してくれます。 seedance2.0 @TopviewAIhq prompt 15-second continuous single-shot action sequence. No cuts. No scene transitions. Japanese-style anime action aesthetics, ultra-detailed, cinematic quality, with a cool color palette of cold whites, pale blues, and subtle neon reflections. Floating debris suspended in the air, dynamic motion, dramatic lighting, high-end anime film look. Character: A petite anime girl with long silver-white hair styled in twin low sections, soft layered strands flowing weightlessly, with subtle iridescent color streaks (cyan, magenta, yellow accents). Large teal-green eyes, calm and emotionless with a faint hint of softness. Wearing a black futuristic tactical jacket covered in multicolor glitch-like paint patterns (RGB splashes), high collar partially covering her mouth, multiple belts, pouches, and mechanical details. Equipped with sleek high-tech headphones with glowing accents. Slim build, delicate but precise movements. Weapon: A minimalist black katana with subtle reflective edge, clean and elegant design. Scene: Interior of a white, ethereal zero-gravity space. The environment is minimalistic and surreal, with soft glowing light, broken gravity, floating fragments of panels and geometric debris drifting slowly. The space feels silent, vast, and dreamlike. 0–3s — tension Camera drifts slowly in zero gravity. The girl floats in mid-air, hair and jacket drifting softly. She holds the katana loosely, body relaxed but ready. Tiny debris particles pass by in slow motion. 3–6s — ignition Enemy figures (abstract humanoid silhouettes, minimal detail) push off floating surfaces and rush toward her from multiple directions. She slightly tilts her head, eyes tracking movement precisely. 6–10s — chaos choreography Combat begins in full 360° zero-gravity space. She spins gracefully mid-air, body rotating fluidly while executing precise katana strikes. Each slash is clean and controlled, leaving faint neon trails (cool blue/purple). Camera follows her in a smooth tumbling motion, synchronized with her rotation, maintaining cinematic clarity. 10–13s — escalation She lightly touches a floating fragment with her foot, then forcefully launches herself forward. High-speed dash across multiple enemies in a straight, elegant line. Time slightly compresses, emphasizing speed and precision. 13–15s — final frame Motion slows down. Enemy bodies drift apart silently in zero gravity. She stabilizes her posture, floating upright. Hair and jacket settle gently. She lowers the katana slightly. Final frame holds on her calm expression in the white void. Freeze.

prompts.text: BREAKING: Someone just built a FREE alternative to Screen Studio. No subscription. No watermark. No limits. And it covers what most people actually use. Here is what OpenScreen is. Screen Studio costs $29/month. OpenScreen costs $0. Forever. No trial tricks. No upsell. Just a clean, open-source screen recorder built for product demos. Because most people do not need 50 features. They need this: → Smooth zooms → Clean backgrounds → Simple editing → Professional-looking output That is exactly what OpenScreen focuses on. Here is what it can do: → Record full screen or specific windows → Auto zoom or manual zoom with full control → Record mic + system audio together → Add motion blur for smooth transitions → Customize backgrounds, colors, gradients → Add text, arrows, and annotations → Trim clips and adjust speed per segment → Export in multiple formats and aspect ratios And yes... it works everywhere: → macOS → Windows → Linux No ecosystem lock-in. No forced upgrades. But here is the real shift. This is not trying to replace Screen Studio. It is redefining the category. Most users are paying $348/year for features they never touch. OpenScreen strips it down to what actually matters. Clean demos. Fast workflow. Zero cost. Which means: For a huge chunk of creators... Paid screen recording tools just became optional.

prompts.text: *“Anime cooking scene, Makoto Shinkai cinematic style, side-angle camera. Flour-dusted wooden table, sunlight streaming through window, all ingredients arranged in bowls: tomato sauce, cherry tomatoes, salami, mushrooms, peppers, shredded mozzarella, parmesan, fresh basil, olive oil bottle, dried oregano, red pepper flakes. Hands pick up dough ball, begin stretching it sideways — dough pulls and stretches thin, flour dust lifts into sunlight in slow motion. Hands place stretched dough flat on floured surface, grab wooden rolling pin — roll firmly forward and back, dough spreads wide, edges curl slightly. Hands lift dough round, lay it flat. Spoon scoops tomato sauce from red bowl, pours onto center of dough — back of spoon spreads it in slow confident spiral outward, sauce glistens. Hands drizzle olive oil bottle in thin stream over sauce — golden drops catch sunlight. Right hand grabs shredded mozzarella by the handful, scatters generously — cheese lands softly across surface. Hands layer toppings one by one: salami slices placed in overlapping circle, mushroom slices dropped casually, colorful pepper pieces scattered, cherry tomatoes halved and pressed gently into cheese. Fingers pinch dried oregano from jar, crumble over pizza — fine herb dust floats in light rays. Pinch of red pepper flakes scattered. Fresh basil leaves torn by hand, placed last. Hands slide pizza onto wooden peel. Camera pulls back — full pizza visible, golden afternoon light, flour particles glowing, volumetric light rays through window. Rich layered sounds throughout: dough stretching, rolling pin on wood, sauce spreading, cheese scattering, toppings dropping. 15 seconds, smooth flowing pacing, ultra-detailed food animation.”* Step 5: Click Generate and wait for the result

prompts.text: The camera rockets up from below, tearing past jagged cliff walls, then whips into a downward tilt to reveal the ocean below in full violent eruption, waves detonating against black rock. On the cliff edge stands a warrior woman, utterly fearless, gazing into the maelstrom as if daring it to rise higher. She steps forward without hesitation and leaps off the edge, arms spread wide, plummeting through howling wind and salt spray. The camera dives alongside her in freefall, her hair and cloak thrashing upward as the black rocks and churning sea rush closer. Then a massive shadow tears through the mist below — a dragon surges upward with wings unfurling like storm sails, scales gleaming wet with ocean spray. It swoops beneath her mid-fall, and she lands on its back with practiced grace, fingers gripping the ridges along its neck. The dragon banks hard, climbing in a spiraling arc back up past the cliff face. The camera keeps pace, circling tight, then pulls ahead into a final close, frontal reveal: she rides the beast skyward, wind-whipped, calm, and burning with determination, the raging ocean now small and powerless far below.

Pairing the raw generation power of Seedance 2.0 with Topview Agent's smart automation is unlocking some crazy long-form video workflows.

prompts.text: A towering humanoid entity made of shifting translucent geometry appears above a megacity skyline, its body composed of overlapping dimensional planes reflecting alternate realities — hook at second two: the entity opens both hands and a vertical dimensional tear slices through the city. Reality splits. Two versions of the city begin occupying the same space. Buildings phase through each other as competing timelines collide. The destruction is dimensional and structural: streets fold into mirrored corridors, cars fall sideways into parallel gravity planes, and skyscrapers fragment into geometric shards as their alternate versions misalign. Every movement of the entity widens the rift. Entire districts slide into adjacent dimensions. The gauntlet unfolds through a skyline progressively divided between worlds: street level where pedestrians phase between realities → mid-rise districts where buildings overlap in transparent duplicates → rooftop where entire towers rotate into perpendicular universes. Aircraft vanish into dimensional folds. Bridges twist into impossible angles. Chase-cam flying through intersecting city layers as gravity shifts between realities. Velocity ramp when the dimensional rift expands wide enough to swallow an entire city block. Cut to aerial: the megacity now exists as two overlapping worlds drifting apart. The dimensional entity stands between them. Diegetic prismatic dimensional light reflecting through fractured architecture and overlapping skylines, cinematic multiverse distortion effects, particle fragments of shattered reality, 4K. prompts.text: So I've been running Seedance 2.0 inside @lovart_ai and instead of just doing text-to-video, I wanted to build my own character and product from scratch using Nano Banana 2. For now let's call the product RAWFUEL. Sounds a bit weird, I know, but this is just for presentation purposes LOL One thing I really love about LovartAI is how manageable it keeps everything. One project page for everything. And because I use the Omni-Reference approach a lot, I end up generating a ton of sheets, from character sheets, location sheets, props sheets, object sheets. One thing people always bring up is that Seedance 2.0 struggles with real faces. I guess the trick is to simply not use close-up face references, because it will be detected as a real human face and Seedance doesn't allow that (for now). So stick to the three-view full-body format. My guess perhaps it gets detected as a 3D render instead? I don't know, just guessing lol. But it holds up surprisingly well. Here are the two sheets I generated: Character Sheet (@ IMAGE 1) photorealism, character sheet, three-view, full figure, white background, Indonesian woman, high ponytail hairstyle with three chrome hair clips, oversized black zip hoodie worn open, dark charcoal compression top underneath, black wide-leg cargo pants with contrast white stitching on the seams, chunky black and white low-top sneakers, thick silver chain necklace, small silver hoop earrings, dark moody attitude, front view center, side profile left, back view right, clean studio lighting with hard shadows, no background elements, full body head to toe. Product Sheet (@ IMAGE 2) photorealism, product shot, character sheet layout, three-view, white background, energy drink can, matte black aluminum can with brushed texture, tall slim format, bold neon orange and acid green graphic design on dark ground, large aggressive stylized text reading RAWFUEL across the center band, jagged electric bolt icon above the text, distressed glitch-pattern texture wrapping the can body, subtle condensation droplets on the lower third, front view center, 45-degree angle view left, back label view right, hard directional studio lighting, deep cast shadows, high contrast, no lens flare, commercial product photography style.

prompts.text: Microdrama in your head? Seedance brings it out. (created with Seedance 2.0 on WaveSpeedAI) Available here: https:// wavespeed.ai/models/bytedan ce/seedance-2.0/text-to-video?ref=shaochiu ... Prompt share: Scene (0–5s): A stormy suburban American street at night, rain pouring, streetlights flickering. Close-up of a 7-year-old blond boy (soaked, cheeks flushed) crouching down, gently picking up a shivering stray orange cat. The cat meows weakly, clinging to his chest. Smash cut — the front door bursts open as the boy rushes into a warm yet tense living room. His mother (wearing a home knit sweater, stern expression) stands at the staircase, her face darkening. Dialogue 1 (Mom, furious and sharp): Are you kidding me?! You brought a dirty stray into MY house?! Scene (6–10s): The boy clutches the cat tightly, eyes tearing up, trying to explain. The cat suddenly stops shaking, slowly lifting its head, pupils shrinking unnaturally. In an instant — the orange cat LEAPS straight onto the mother’s face. Slow motion: claws latch onto her face, she lets out a suffocating scream, stumbling backward, knocking over furniture. The boy rushes forward in panic, desperately pulling at the cat. Dialogue 2 (Boy, breaking down): Stop! Stop! Let her go!! PLEASE!! Scene (11–15s): Extreme close-up — the boy finally yanks the cat off. Silence. The mother’s “face” peels off completely, along with her blonde hair, sliding down. Revealing — a green-skinned, large black-eyed alien face, wet and cold, staring directly forward. The boy’s pupils shake violently. The “face” drops from his hands onto the floor. The orange cat lands and slowly turns its head, eerily calm. The alien tilts its head slightly, mouth stretching into an unnatural smile. Dialogue 3 (Alien, low and eerie): ...You weren’t supposed to see that. Sound Design: Heavy rain and distant thunder at the start, door slam impact; mid-section tearing/claw impact, furniture crashing, suffocating screams; final section sudden silence → wet peeling sound → rising low-frequency horror tone, final line with subtle echo. Duration: Exactly 15 seconds (high-intensity American micro drama, dense shock beats, extreme twist payoff) #sd2 #seedance #CatsLover #aivideo

prompts.text: The 5 AI demos that broke the internet this week: 1. Claude Code got computer use Claude can now open apps, click through UIs, use the browser, and test code from the terminal. This is one of the clearest signs that AI agents are moving from chat to real software work. 2. Gemma 4 launched Google dropped its newest open models with long context, multimodal support, and commercial use. Open-weight models keep getting closer to what most people thought only frontier labs could ship. 3. OpenCode blew up An open-source Claude Code-style coding agent that works across multiple LLMs. One of the hottest workflows in AI just got a lot more remixable. 4. Seedance 2.0 pushed AI video forward Stronger motion, more control, and much more usable outputs. AI video keeps moving from “cool demo” to “actual creative tool.” 5. Anton feels like an AI coworker It connects to your data, writes code on the fly, builds dashboards, and remembers context. Closer to “AI employee” than “chatbot.” AI is moving so fast that most people miss the biggest launches entirely. Bookmark this. Next week’s list will look completely different.

prompts.author: @sharbel
prompts.tweet_text:
The 5 AI demos that broke the internet this week:

1. Claude Code got computer use
Claude can now open apps, click through UIs, use the browser, and test code from the terminal.
This is one of the clearest signs that AI agents are moving from chat to real software work.

2. Gemma 4 launched
Google dropped its newest open models with long context, multimodal support, and commercial use.
Open-weight models keep getting closer to what most people thought only frontier labs could ship.

3. OpenCode blew up
An open-source Claude Code-style coding agent that works across multiple LLMs.
One of the hottest workflows in AI just got a lot more remixable.

4. Seedance 2.0 pushed AI video forward
Stronger motion, more control, and much more usable outputs.
AI video keeps moving from “cool demo” to “actual creative tool.”

5. Anton feels like an AI coworker
It connects to your data, writes code on the fly, builds dashboards, and remembers context.
Closer to “AI employee” than “chatbot.”

AI is moving so fast that most people miss the biggest launches entirely.

Bookmark this.

Next week’s list will look completely different.

prompts.text: An anonymous model named HappyHorse-1.0 topped the Video Arena leaderboard on the AI video evaluation platform Artificial Analysis last week, securing first place in both the text-to-video and image-to-video tracks (no-audio category), displacing ByteDance's Seedance 2.0 to second place. In the audio-included category, Seedance 2.0 still holds a slight lead. No press conference, no technical blog, no company attribution—nobody has publicly claimed ownership to date. The Video Arena rankings are based on an Elo blind testing system, where users vote on the better of two generated videos without knowing the models' identities. HappyHorse has been on the leaderboard for a short time, with about 3,500 comparison samples—less than half of Seedance 2.0's—and a wider confidence interval (±12-13 points), but its lead in the no-audio track (about 76 points in text-to-video, about 48 points in image-to-video) still far exceeds the margin of error. Judging from the website's language order (Chinese and Cantonese ahead of English) and the "HappyHorse" nod to the 2026 Year of the Horse, the industry speculates the model comes from a Chinese team. Two prevailing theories: 1. Multiple industry self-media outlets claim the model originates from Alibaba's TaoTian Group's Future Life Lab, led by director Zhang Di. Zhang previously served as vice president of technology at Kuaishou, and starting in 2024, he spearheaded the development of Keling AI, releasing Keling 2.0 Master Edition in April 2025, before returning to Alibaba in November of the same year 2. X user Vigo Zhao conducted an item-by-item comparison and found that HappyHorse matches the open-source daVinci-MagiHuman from AI video startup http:// Sand.ai (released in March this year) across multiple benchmark metrics, with highly similar website structures as well. http:// Sand.ai was founded by Cao Yue, the first author of Swin Transformer, and is dubbed by the industry as the "DeepSeek of AI video" The HappyHorse website states the model has 15 billion parameters, a 40-layer self-attention Transformer using the Transfusion architecture (which unifies text autoregressive prediction and video-audio diffusion generation within the same model), 8-step inference, and outputs 1080p video with synchronized audio, supporting lip-sync in seven languages: Chinese, English, Japanese, Korean, German, French, and Cantonese. It's fully open-source and permits commercial use.

prompts.text: Create a 4-second ultra-cute vertical animated character video. A tiny fluffy purple puppy-like creature with soft fur, a round face, big glossy expressive eyes, a small black nose, short floppy ears, and adorable baby proportions. The style is polished soft 3D animation, very cute, smooth fur, pastel purple background, soft studio lighting. The character walks forward slowly with natural body bounce and soft paw movement, first looking slightly away with an innocent expression. Then it turns its head and upper body toward the camera, gives a surprised but cheeky look, and raises one hand to show one middle finger clearly. It then turns forward again for a brief beat as if continuing to walk, then quickly looks back at the camera with a more mischievous expression and raises both hands, showing two middle fingers clearly at the same time. End on the double gesture for a brief moment so it is readable. Focus on smooth body plasticity, head turn, shoulder turn, eye movement, hand gesture clarity, and cute facial expression. The eyes must be large, shiny, and very expressive. The fur must look soft and detailed. The motion must be funny, cute, and playful, not aggressive. No text on screen. No captions. No overlays. Visual style: ultra-cute polished 3D character animation, soft pastel lighting, clean background, meme-ready, adorable but sassy. No realism, no horror, no ugly face, no distorted eyes, no extra fingers, no extra limbs, no blurry motion, no low-detail fur, no dark background, no aggressive tone, no human body proportions.

prompts.text: Yeah, multimodal references are a huge leap forward for AI video. Single prompts often feel like throwing darts in the dark—great for quick ideas, but creators need control, consistency, and iteration. Dreamina Seedance 2.0 gets that: feeding in images, text, and instructions to remix a viral donkey-on-motorcycle meme into a full ad-style sequence (barn escape, donuts, mountain jumps with slogan) shows real workflow alignment. This is the future—shifting from "prompt and pray" to true collaboration. Tools like this will dominate as they scale. What's your take on the next evolution?
